title: Infrastructure as Prompt (IaP) v1
description: Machine-readable schema for IaP documents. IaP describes what infrastructure should exist (intent), never how a provider implements it. Specification version 1.3.0. Fields without an x-iap-since annotation have been present since 1.0.0.

apiVersion.description: IaP group and major version this document conforms to. Documents pin the major version only; minor revisions of the specification are strictly additive.

profiles.description: Named overlays applied at plan time via RFC 7386 JSON Merge Patch. Environments are profiles. Exactly one profile is active per plan.

resources.description: Flat map of every declared resource. The map key is the resource identifier, unique within the document.

relationships.description: Selector-based rule edges only. Point-to-point relationships MUST be declared inline on the source resource.

policies.description: Declarative governance rules evaluated against the canonical (profile-merged) document.

compliance.description: Document-level compliance framework scope declarations. Frameworks activate their registered policy bundles.

outputs.description: Named exports of abstract resource attributes (never provider identifiers).

resourceId.description: DNS-label grammar. Resource identifiers are the keys of the resources map and the only way to reference a resource.

quantity.description: Kubernetes-style quantity grammar for exact capacity intent (e.g. 100Gi, 2, 500m).

duration.description: Duration grammar: integer followed by ms, s, m, h, or d.

labels.description: Free-form key/value labels used by selectors, policies, and rule edges. Values are always strings.

name.description: Document name, unique within an organization.

annotations.description: Free-form string annotations. Never semantic: tools MUST NOT change behavior based on annotations.

availability.description: Availability intent as a measurable SLO floor: standard >= 99.9% (single-zone acceptable), high >= 99.95% (multi-zone), maximum >= 99.99% (multi-region-capable).

exposure.description: Network reachability intent: public (internet-reachable), internal (organization network only), private (reachable only via declared connectsTo relationships).

size.description: Portable t-shirt sizing for compute capacity. Providers map sizes to concrete instance classes; exact quantities may be given via resources overrides.

encryption.description: Encryption intent. Omission never weakens posture: both dimensions default to required.

observability.description: Cross-cutting observability intent available on every resource.

computeResources.description: Optional exact-quantity override of t-shirt sizing.

reference.description: Image reference, source repository URL, or archive location.

relationshipType.description: Closed v1 relationship verb set. Extensions may add edge attributes, never verbs. All verbs except dependsOn imply an ordering dependency (target before source); replicatesTo is symmetric-capable and implies no ordering.

relationshipEdge.description: Inline edge declared on the source resource. The declaring resource is always the source.

access.description: Access level asserted by this edge (connectsTo/storesDataIn only). Least privilege is derived from these values. Default: read-write.

selector.description: Label selector matching resources in the same document.

ruleEdge.description: Selector-based bulk edge: every resource matched by source gains this relationship to target.

extends.description: Name of one other profile whose overrides are applied first (root of the extends chain first).

overrides.description: RFC 7386 JSON Merge Patch applied to the document: objects deep-merge, arrays replace wholesale, null deletes. Profiles may add resources. The merged result MUST itself be a valid IaP document.

policyCondition.description: Deterministic structural condition tree. No embedded expression language in v1.

field.description: Dot path into the resource (e.g. spec.encryption.atRest).

value.description: Comparison value; required for all operators except exists and absent.

effect.description: deny: violation fails validation. warn: violation is reported. require: the rule MUST hold; tools MAY offer a deterministic autofix.

extensions.description: Namespaced, non-normative provider refinements. Extension Non-Interference Rule: deleting every extensions block from a valid document MUST yield a valid document with identical core semantics. Unknown namespaces warn, never fail.

version.description: Extension package version (document level registration only).

attribute.description: Abstract attribute name declared by the resource kind (e.g. endpoint, connectionSecret, identifier).

resilienceBackupRequired.description: Data protection and recovery intent. backup defaults to "required" for stateful kinds (Database, Volume).

resilienceBackupPreferred.description: Data protection and recovery intent. backup defaults to "preferred" for durable-by-design kinds (ObjectStore).

Application.description: Logical grouping of resources that ship together. x-iap-capability: application

runtime.description: Execution substrate hint; largely a mapping concern. Value "kubernetes" is available since 1.3.0 (x-iap-since; JSON Schema cannot annotate individual enum values).

configuration.description: Non-secret configuration values. Secrets MUST be modeled as Secret resources referenced via relationships.

Job.description: Run-to-completion workload, optionally scheduled. x-iap-capability: compute

schedule.description: Cron expression (5-field) or @hourly/@daily/@weekly/@monthly. Omit for on-demand jobs.

concurrency.description: Behavior when a new run starts while a previous run is active.

Function.description: Event-invoked workload; triggers derive from consumesFrom/routesTo relationships. x-iap-capability: compute

Gateway.description: Traffic entry point; routes are routesTo relationships with path/host attributes. x-iap-capability: network

exposure.description: Gateways exist to expose traffic; private is not a valid gateway exposure.

protocol.description: Application protocol the gateway terminates and routes. Provider-neutral protocol/query-language names, never provider products. Optional and carries NO default, so existing Gateway documents canonicalize byte-identically.

certificate.description: Reference to a Certificate resource; omit for provider-managed certificates.

Database.description: Managed database intent; engine names are open, non-proprietary dialects the application code depends on. x-iap-capability: database

class.description: Data model the application depends on. Values "wide-column" and "warehouse" are available since 1.1.0 (x-iap-since; JSON Schema cannot annotate individual enum values).

engine.description: Wire-protocol/dialect intent. Provider products never appear here.

ObjectStore.description: Durable blob/object storage intent. x-iap-capability: storage

Volume.description: Block/file volume attached to compute. x-iap-capability: storage

maxReceives.description: Default applies only while enabled is true; authored alongside enabled: false it is an IAP104 error.

Topic.description: Publish/subscribe channel; subscribers declare consumesFrom edges. x-iap-capability: messaging

Identity.description: Identity intent; for workload identities concrete permissions derive from relationship access levels. x-iap-capability: identity

type.description: Identity category. workload: a machine identity whose permissions derive from edge access levels. user-directory: a directory of human/end-user identities (e.g. an authentication user pool), distinct from workload identity. Value "user-directory" is available since 1.3.0 (x-iap-since; JSON Schema cannot annotate individual enum values).

Secret.description: Managed secret material; values never appear in IaP documents. x-iap-capability: security

source.description: generated: the platform creates the value. provided: supplied out-of-band at deploy time. external: synchronized from an external secret system.

Certificate.description: TLS certificate material and issuance intent; referenced by Gateway.spec.tls.certificate. x-iap-capability: security

domains.description: DNS names the certificate covers; wildcard prefix (*.) permitted; entries MUST be unique.

issuance.description: managed: the platform obtains and renews the certificate. imported: material is supplied out-of-band (e.g. via a Secret); the platform never generates keys.

keyAlgorithm.description: Key algorithm intent; algorithm names, never provider products.

DnsZone.description: Authoritative DNS zone intent; record-level intent is reserved for a future minor. x-iap-capability: network

zoneName.description: Fully qualified apex name of the zone (no wildcard, no trailing dot).

visibility.description: public: resolvable from the internet. internal: resolvable only inside the organization network (split-horizon). Scopes name resolution, not workload reachability (contrast exposure).

dnssec.description: DNSSEC signing intent. required: the mapping MUST sign the zone or fail closed; preferred: sign where the substrate supports it; none: unsigned.

Registry.description: Artifact and container-image registry intent; push/pull access derives from connectsTo edge access levels. x-iap-capability: storage

format.description: Artifact format the registry stores; aligns with the artifact.type grammar (minus source).

immutability.description: enabled: a stored artifact version or tag can never be overwritten.

Dashboard.description: Curated observability dashboard over telemetry delivered by monitoredBy edges. Fields deliberately carry no defaults: canonicalization of pre-1.1.0 documents is unchanged. x-iap-capability: observability

signals.description: Telemetry signal classes; omitted means every signal delivered by incident monitoredBy edges.

Alert.description: Notification rule and routing intent evaluated over telemetry delivered by monitoredBy edges. Fields deliberately carry no defaults: canonicalization of pre-1.1.0 documents is unchanged. x-iap-capability: observability

severity.description: Severity classification of the notifications this alert emits.

severityFloor.description: Minimum severity of evaluated conditions that triggers notification; omitted means notify at every severity.

signals.description: Telemetry signal classes; omitted means every signal delivered by incident monitoredBy edges.

channels.description: Provider-neutral notification channel classes; concrete destinations are mapping/extension territory.

Network.description: Explicit network segmentation and topology intent beyond what exposure and connectsTo edges derive. x-iap-capability: network

scope.description: Geographic span of the network's address space. regional: a single region; multi-region: spans regions (globally routable intent).

tiers.description: Reachability tiers the network provides. public: internet-facing segments; private: internally routable with egress; isolated: no egress.

addressSpace.description: Optional exact IPv4 CIDR block intent (e.g. 10.0.0.0/16); omitted means the platform allocates a non-overlapping range. A neutral IP concept, never a provider product.

Stream.description: Ordered, replayable event stream with consumer-managed offsets; distinct from Topic fan-out delivery. x-iap-capability: messaging

retention.description: How long records remain available for replay (duration grammar). Typed inline, not via the common duration $ref, so a reserved-era Stream's authored spelling is preserved byte-for-byte in canonicalization (no defaults or unit re-spelling).

ordering.description: none: no cross-record ordering guarantee; partition: order preserved within a partition key.

throughput.description: Sustained ingest capacity intent (records per second or megabits per second).

Workflow.description: Multi-step orchestration of Job and Function executions with state transitions. x-iap-capability: compute

steps.description: Declared number of orchestrated steps (advisory); the concrete step graph is defined out-of-band, and a step grammar may be added additively in a later minor.

execution.description: standard: durable, long-running, full execution history; express: high-volume, short-lived, best-effort history.

timeout.description: Maximum wall-clock time for a single workflow execution (duration grammar). Typed inline, not via the common duration $ref, so a reserved-era Workflow's authored spec is preserved byte-for-byte in canonicalization.

SearchIndex.description: Full-text or vector search index over application data. x-iap-capability: database

indexType.description: text: full-text/relevance search; vector: similarity search over embeddings.

Cdn.description: Content delivery / edge distribution intent: caches and serves content from edge locations in front of one or more origins. Provider-neutral; realized later by an edge/CDN service. x-iap-capability: network

exposure.description: Edge reachability. public: internet-facing distribution. internal: reachable only within the organization network.

origins.description: Backend origins the edge distributes. Each target names an in-document resource (typically a Service, Gateway, or ObjectStore). Referential integrity of target is advisory in 1.3.0 (no generic spec-field reference check yet); authors SHOULD also declare a dependsOn edge to each origin for ordering.

pathPattern.description: Optional path prefix routed to this origin (e.g. /static). Omitted: this origin serves all paths.

tls.description: TLS posture at the edge; mirrors Gateway.spec.tls.

certificate.description: Reference to a Certificate resource; omit for provider-managed certificates.

mode.description: standard: cache per origin/response directives. aggressive: cache broadly, favoring hit rate. disabled: pass through to the origin, no edge caching.

EventBus.description: Event routing intent: accepts events from sources and routes them to targets by declarative rules. Provider-neutral; realized later by an event-routing service. x-iap-capability: messaging

sources.description: Classes of event sources the bus accepts. internal: in-organization workloads. partner: third-party/SaaS integrations. custom: application-defined event producers. Neutral source classes, never provider products.

rules.description: Declarative routing rules. Targets are wired as routesTo relationships from the bus (the closed verb set is unchanged); each rule matches events and the bus forwards matches to the routed targets.

eventPattern.description: Open matching object over event attributes; neutral and provider-agnostic. Absent: the rule matches every event.

schemaRegistry.description: Whether the bus enforces a managed event-schema registry. none: no enforcement. managed: events are validated against a managed schema registry.

retention.description: How long undelivered/replayable events are retained.

ReservedKind.description: Reserved registry kind. The reserved registry is EMPTY as of 1.2.0: all nine kinds reserved in 1.0.0 have graduated to fully specified kinds — Certificate, DnsZone, Registry, Dashboard, Alert in 1.1.0 (IEP-0015), and Network, Stream, Workflow, SearchIndex in 1.2.0 (IEP-0016). This definition is retained as the reusable loose-spec template so a future minor MAY reserve new kind names; the reserved-fallback dispatch branch in $defs/resource.allOf was removed (JSON Schema forbids an empty enum, and every kindName now has its own dispatch branch) and is re-added when a future minor reserves a new name. When it does, validators MUST accept the reserved kind and SHOULD warn (IAP801).
